title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Compiler Case: Diagnostics Evidence
summary: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts diagnostics evidence increment in a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read diagnostic codes, primary locations, related information, and causal type relationships before changing code.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
